Discovery: BlueBird satellites 8, 9, and 10 launch on SpaceX Falcon 9 is scheduled for June 17 2026 — two days away; success could enable initial commercial services by July; Q1 2026 revenue up 1952% YoY with $1.2B in contracted commercial commitments and TELUS partnership confirmed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Launch June 17 is genuine de-risking but 9-analyst consensus $80 is 3% BELOW current $82.41 — at these levels you are chasing consensus, not buying value

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: 87% SI is STRUCTURAL — shorts focused on BP/Shell open arbitrations (existential contingent liabilities); Repsol won Jan 2026, Edison settled March 2026, but BP/Shell remain open; Q1 revenue $4.6B +59%; FCF -$9.3B construction burn; IPO governance risk (Tier 2: YF, Fintel)

Discovery: Venture Global recently completed its IPO and is ramping up Calcasieu Pass 2 LNG capacity; it is the fastest-growing US LNG exporter and operates at a lower cost structure than Cheniere, making it a higher-beta play on the same Qatari supply gap — still less covered by institutional investors than LNG and at an earlier stage of contracted ramp.
growth_picks.analyst_take: BP/Shell arbitrations are open multi-billion contingent liabilities making 87% short interest a structural thesis, not a squeeze setup; wait for legal clarity before sizing

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: $887M Army drone contracts + $117M Army June 2026 + Taiwan UAS. BUT: securities class action deadline July 27 over $1.7B SCAR termination and $151M goodwill impairment; negative op/net margins; June 29 earnings binary under live litigation (Tier 2: TipRanks, GlobeNewswire)

Discovery: Ukraine approved a record UAH 4.4 trillion ($97.6B) defense budget for 2026 with heavy weight on drones, while the US Army issued a $20B IDIQ to Anduril (private) — the residual demand for battle-proven loitering munitions falls on public players like AeroVironment, which operates in a de facto duopoly for small-UAS and Switchblade munitions and guided FY2026 revenue to $1.85-1.95B.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense drone moat is real but securities class action (deadline July 27) and Space Force SCAR termination damaged management credibility; earnings June 29 is binary with litigation overhang — wait for both to clear

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: SI 17.7% UP 230% over 12 months — shorts adding. New CFO July 9, new CMO (operational rebuilding under crisis); Q1 guided only 10% growth; Amazon gaining CTV share; 3 of 6 big holdcos relationships impaired; earnings deceleration accelerating (Tier 2: Fintel, AdExchanger)
growth_picks.analyst_take: Down 79% from ATH but moat actively eroding (Amazon CTV share gains, broken agency relationships); short interest UP 230% over 12 months as shorts add — not a squeeze, a thesis; wait for new CFO's first guidance print

avoid_picks.analyst_take: Labor-hours billing model is the direct casualty of AI coding adoption (Cursor enterprise partnership = clients automating EPAM's own engineers); 25.7% short interest is institutional consensus on structural decline; 4.5-7.5% FY2026 growth guidance at $95 = value trap even at 6.8x Fwd P/E

Hormuz MOU signed June 19 removes the entire rate thesis — product tanker rates at $70K/day normalize post-reopening; stock near 52W high with the catalyst about to be removed; fair value $55-60 post-normalization

AI disruption casualty with no recovery path: Q4 2025 revenue -49% YoY, market cap $156M from $14.7B peak, 45% workforce cut, two simultaneous disruption vectors (ChatGPT substitution + Google traffic collapse)
